title: anthyllis boissieri
intro: Anthyllis boissieri, also known as Boissier's kidney vetch or Boissier's vetch, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its diuretic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which have been found to be beneficial in treating kidney stones, urinary tract infections, and skin conditions such as eczema. The plant is rich in flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties, including reducing inflammation and promoting the expulsion of kidney stones. In herbal preparations, Anthyllis boissieri is often combined with other herbs to create teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be used to alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
